element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Fundamental controls established before combat elements
2v2 full court basketball against selected team
Central distinguishing mechanic - free punching without referee penalty
Direct ball theft and 'flying leap' tackle maneuver
Environmental obstacles cause ballhandler to fall, enabling steals
Collision with referee on court causes fall, similar to hazard objects
1-minute sudden death periods until next basket scored

typical_relief_triggers: Shot clock violation call (only penalty enforced)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Arcade pace with constant action-reaction cycles; 4 quarters of 4 minutes each create structured rhythm with escalating intensity toward final buzzer

difficulty_curve.early_game: Immediate full complexity - all mechanics (punching, stealing, hazards) available from start; 2v2 full court basketball with no tutorial progression
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Sustained arcade challenge with increasing opponent aggressiveness and court hazard frequency as quarters progress
difficulty_curve.late_game: Quarter 4 escalation toward final buzzer; sudden death overtime adds binary win/lose tension with no score mercy
notable_spikes: Transition to overtime (1-minute sudden death cycles)
Genesis version: 95% shot success rate glitch creates paradoxical difficulty (excessive ease)

notable_level_patterns.description: Standard 4-quarter game (4 minutes per quarter) with home/away team selection. All matches follow same spatial/temporal structure. Hazards and referee placement randomized.

Court obstacles (soda cans, candy wrappers, referee) placed on floor to impede ballhandler movement, causing fall and enabling defensive steal. Environmental punishment mechanic replaces traditional foul system.
Tie after regulation triggers 1-minute overtime periods repeating until next basket. Binary outcome per possession cycle creates escalating tension.
